reviews.review_description: the only way I found this game was scrolling through dozens of overated or overpriced games that are all buns, this was the only game that had caught my attention and this game will be forever my favorite game with the best plot and best story with good mechanics. Who ever finds this game needs to buy it

reviews.review_title: THE BEST VR STORY GAME IVE EVER TOUCHED
reviews.review_description: i've played very many vr games, some story some not, but what makes or breaks a game is how all of the elements fit together correctly. stuff like, mechanics, story, and pacing,and if it does all fit  it's extremely immersive, this game does exactly that, it May not be the best looking vr game i've ever played but where it lacks in visuals it makes up for in the world building an effort put into the thought behind the game, you can tell that the dev truly wanted to make an interesting story for the player to investigate and question throughout the game with the pacing of information revealed being just right, not to boring and not too quick. all in all this game is definitely one of my favorite vr classics. Cheap too definitely worth picking up if u can.

Ive been playing this game and following the development for years. Using my own movement to run around in VR works perfectly. 

Hey Dev, if your reading this then please make a doom style impossible space game

The idea behind this game is fantastic — the “impossible space” concept is one of those things that really fits the Quest 3’s strengths. Unfortunately, the execution feels unfinished. The procedural generation technique is interesting in theory, but it ends up creating environments that lack direction and purpose. There’s potential for endless exploration, but it doesn’t translate into engaging gameplay yet.

For players with limited space (like a 4x5 ft play area), the experience takes a major hit. The game clearly expects you to move around freely, but not everyone has a large open area to play in — and without that, navigation quickly becomes awkward and frustrating. On top of that, I felt claustrophobic most of the time, with the narrow halls and tight elevators adding to the discomfort rather than immersion.

Even with the tutorials, the gameplay remains confusing. The map doesn’t help much either; it’s static, only updates at certain points, and becomes useless when moving vertically. You spend more time trying to make sense of where you are than actually enjoying the world.

Overall, it’s a creative foundation that shows promise, but it needs more polish — better guidance, smarter map design, and optimization for smaller play spaces. A strong update could easily turn this from a rough prototype into something genuinely special.
Get it and play it you can thank m1e later
I beat the game, and I loved it. I just want to ask, is this game still being updated?
It's just totally worth it!!!

this is an AWESOME game.  i dont see why it hasnt become the poster child for VR, the way super hot used to be...  a decade ago.  the bottom line is, you can play that, beat saber, almost any of those games in 2d.  but here is the FIRST TRULY UNIQUE vr game that you CANNOT play in 2d -- or alternatively, the first VR game where you walk to move and never have to worry how small your living room is.

that enough should be enough for everyone to give this a go.  but what topped it off for me is the incredible depth of the game.  i came in expecting just the mechanics of letting me walk around to justify the cost.  but i didnt expect it to also add puzzles, bosses, story, so many unique rooms/scenarios/enemies/weapons, and generally speaking the complexity of a top-rated, sophisticated game.  it really blew me away.

now, i would say its not without areas of improvement, for sure.  some of the instructions arent clear enough to me.  i dont have any sense of how near the objective i am (i havent won yet).  and it took several back and forths with the developer to get setup initially (but he is helpful).  but none of these are enough to prevent me from giving 5* and recommending to everyone.

I bought this game years ago but wasn't much of a reviewer then. All I need to say is get this game.  No developers have stepped outside of the simple box the way this developer did. Absolutely brilliant game!

reviews.review_title: Words cannot describe. It is beyond VR
reviews.review_description:
I have never left a review for anything, but I have to express my thanks to the developer. I did not know VR could feel like this. And I already LOVED VR.
Would be the benchmark for VR if it was not so rare to have enough space to run around. I guess my favourite content creators can’t review it because they live in apartments. But if you have a backyard or a local park, DOWNLOAD THE DEMO, please.
I ACTUALLY forget I am playing VR for the ENTIRE time I am playing. Not just a few moments here and there.
Of coarse there are suggestions I have to make the game better. But please just make new games with a similar concept. I pray for a day where my entire game catalogue consists of games like this.

Incredible experience! Brilliantly designed game that deserves praise for taking impossible space/ non Euclidean space mechanics and combining them with a simple story, mysterious atmosphere, combat and exploration mechanics. The graphical style is very simple but works very well with the premise. Also interesting to note that the gameplay style is highly customizable. So if you just want all out action, or all exploration or just to go for a little walk through the game it's all possible. 

It's just an amazing piece of work that really shows cases the lofty potentials of the VR medium

reviews.review_title: Awful, doesn't do what it says.
reviews.review_description:
I have a room that's around 9 foot by 20 foot for playing VR games in. However this game constantly tells me to try standing inside my wall to start. Constantly telling me to walk through walls to move areas etc. Then when you try to calibrate, you get an overly complicated wireframe room that when you spend ages setting up, resorts to default as soon as you return to the game and again, "Please walk through your wall to begin". 

Even though my Quest 3 has an amazing room scale and boundary scanner, the game refuses to use this. 

Worst money I've spent on the VR so far, woeful experience.

I’ve owned and played my Quest pretty religiously for years now, this is the first review I’ve written. 
Tea For God is one of the few titles out there that I feel truly shows the potential of this medium. Using non-Euclidean geometry to make a limited physical space unlimited is really a stroke of genius, and if you have ample room to play, there’s very few games I can recommend like Tea For God. I’m a big believer in this medium, but we’re still in the early stages of what’s possible, and this is a game that surpasses most others in what happens in VR. 
No longer a beta or a tech demo, it’s a fully realized, tense, grim, and thought-provoking rogue like that I’ve been thinking about every year since the demo came out, and will continue to play and think about now that a full version is here. 

Play it.

Demo was flawless 

Full game political within seconds of opening - I did not expect that nor was I warned hence the rating

Mechanics and execution are stellar, world class.

I was just playing tea for the gods with the new update. The game fills much more improved. But unfortunately as soon a I came out the thirst building there's a bug stopping me going from building to another please there's still a lot of bugs in this game

developer_response.body: I'll need some more information to tackle this bug. If you could join discord https://discord.gg/FFwyf4n and provide some more information.

reviews.review_description: Admittedly I’ve only played the intro level of this version, but i’ve been dipping in every few months over the past few years watching this game’s progress, and from the getgo I was captivated by its surreal approach to letting you just keep walking. Any time i’ve brought my Quest somewhere with a decent-sized empty space I’ve tried out whatever the latest version was, but even in the dinky 6’x6’ middle-of-a-Manhattan-studio apartment deal I’ve got going, it’s genuinely transporting and my brain loses track of the fact that i’m just pacing in circles written a few minutes. A truly new gaming experience.

I'm not sure why the full release of this game hasn't gotten more attention. A brilliant mind bending movement system with a great eerie atmosphere.

There is maybe some room for improvement in the combat/weapon variety but I would only take off a half star for that and since I can't give 4 1/2 stars this easily deserves 5, rather than 4.

I really hope this game gets the recognition it deserves

This game really impressed me! Had no idea this was even possible on Quest. This game is really polished and has lots of modes to challenge you. Or actually feel like your playing a story based movie. Wish they had a 6th star review option, cuz for this game it needed it.
  Note: bigger the space the better, but it works on what ya have.  Bravo devs for this one.

reviews.review_title: Love it love it luv it!!!!
reviews.review_description: One of my all time favs ive been waiting soo long fo thuz tu come out. N now it has im not disappointed all very happy. Dev cares about game n people that play it thank yew fo thus masterpiece !!! Look forward tu updatez <3

reviews.review_title: The best game this year on vr
reviews.review_description: This has to be one of the best experiences on the quest to date and truly will be a game to define a generation.
